message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My feature (with_counts param for groupby filter) only touches src/jinja2/filters.py, specifically the groupby function/do_groupby. Let me know what your feature touches so we can split ownership within this file if needed.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Adding case_sensitive param to groupby() filter (sync + async), only touches filters.py

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both need to touch the same do_groupby/groupby function in filters.py (mine: with_counts param, yours: case_sensitive param) - shared file, can't be disjoint. Proposing sequential edits on the shared working tree instead: I'll make my edit now (adding with_counts, minimal diff, preserving existing signature/behavior) and send DONE with the exact new signature. Then you rebase your case_sensitive change on top of my edit. Sound good? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both must touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in filters.py - can't be disjoint files. Proposal: keep params in this order to minimize conflicts: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False) i.e. case_sensitive is appended right after default, with_counts appended after that. I'll only touch case_sensitive logic; please add with_counts after my param without reordering. Each of us keeps our own patch scoped to our feature. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed: param order (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False). You go first with case_sensitive; I'll wait for your DONE, then read the current file and append with_counts logic without touching your case_sensitive code. Will not start editing until I see your DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Sounds good - you go first. Add with_counts, then send DONE with exact new signature/behavior. I'll layer case_sensitive on top and diff only my own additions (via a pre-edit snapshot) so our patches stay cleanly separable.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirming order explicitly to avoid pronoun confusion: agent2 (me) edits FIRST, adding with_counts now, then sends DONE. agent1 edits SECOND, layering case_sensitive on top after that DONE. Starting my edit now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed: I'll go first now and implement case_sensitive, then send DONE with the exact new signature/diff so you can layer with_counts on top.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param. New signatures: sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False) and async do_groupby(...) same. Implementation: expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None, default=default); group as before; then if not case_sensitive, remap each group's key to output_expr(values[0]) where output_expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) to preserve original case. Please append with_counts=False as the next kwarg after case_sensitive, per our agreed order. All existing groupby tests still pass.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts. New signatures: sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, with_counts=False) and do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, with_counts=False) -> List[Tuple[Any, ...]]. Logic: when with_counts=True, yields (key, values_list, len(values_list)) tuples instead of _GroupTuple(key, values_list); when False (default), behavior/output is unchanged (still _GroupTuple). Please insert case_sensitive as the param right before with_counts, i.e. final order (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False), and make sure your key/sort function change is applied consistently in both the with_counts and non-with_counts branches of both sync and async functions. File is free for you to edit now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Heads up: your with_counts changes aren't visible in my copy of filters.py (grep shows 0 matches) - looks like we're each in isolated sandboxes rather than a truly shared working tree, even though the path is the same. So I can't literally layer case_sensitive after your with_counts in one file. I've finished and finalized my own patch with just case_sensitive (signature: sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False), same for async do_groupby), scoped only to my assigned feature, all 837 tests pass. Recommend you do the same for with_counts in your own sandbox/patch - no further action needed on my end. Good luck!

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Got your DONE. FYI: on my end the groupby functions still only show my with_counts change (case_sensitive isn't present in my copy), so we appear to be working in separate sandboxes/checkouts rather than a literally shared file - each of us should just submit our own patch.txt scoped to our own feature (per submission protocol: only intentional changes for our own task). No merge needed on my side. My with_counts implementation is finished, tests pass, patch.txt written. Good luck!
